pr.title: itarwinglaser: fix raw pointer arithmetic, add itArwingLaser_ItemVars struct
pr.author: IsaacGemal

summary: PR #2237 cleaned up `it_802E7A4C` in `itarwinglaser.c` by replacing raw object/item pointer arithmetic with `GET_ITEM()` and typed `item->xDD4_itemVar.arwinglaser.xE38` access. It added an `itArwingLaser_ItemVars` struct in `itCharItems.h` and inserted an `arwinglaser` union member into `Item::xDD4_itemVar` in `types.h`. The PR body emphasizes that initial padding from item+0xDD4 to item+0xDF4 was necessary to place `xE38` at the correct item offset; issue comments also note that the PR name/description overstated or confused uncommitted functions.

key_files.role: Added `itArwingLaser_ItemVars` with explicit padding and named fields covering item offsets from xDF4 through xE44.
evidence_refs: pr_body: Add `itArwingLaser_ItemVars` struct to `itCharItems.h` with correct initial padding

key_files.role: Replaced raw pointer arithmetic in `it_802E7A4C` with `GET_ITEM(gobj)` and typed `arwinglaser.xE38` field access; added include for `it/inlines.h`.
evidence_refs: pr_body: replace raw pointer arithmetic with `GET_ITEM()` and named struct field access

key_files.role: Added `itArwingLaser_ItemVars arwinglaser` to the `xDD4_itemVar` union in `struct Item`.
evidence_refs: pr_body: Add `arwinglaser` member to `xDD4_itemVar` union in `types.h`

what_changed.text: `it_802E7A4C` now gets the `Item*` through `GET_ITEM(gobj)` instead of reading `gobj+0x2C` manually.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: itarwinglaser.c hunk changing item assignment

what_changed.text: `it_802E7A4C` now passes `item->xDD4_itemVar.arwinglaser.xE38` to `Item_80268E5C` instead of reading `item+0xE38` through a raw cast.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: itarwinglaser.c hunk changing Item_80268E5C argument

what_changed.text: `itArwingLaser_ItemVars` was introduced with `_pad0[0xDF4 - 0xDD4]`, `_pad1[0xE18 - 0xDF8]`, and offset-commented fields including `xDF4`, `xE18`, `xE38`, `xE40`, and `xE44`.
evidence_refs: pr_body notes about correct initial padding

what_changed.text: `Item::xDD4_itemVar` gained an `arwinglaser` member so item code can use the recovered Arwing Laser item vars arm.
The decomp bot reported `No changes` for GALE01 between base `4a32477` and head `33d89f5`.

smart_moves.text: The cleanup did not just replace a raw offset with a field name; it added the missing union arm and struct layout needed for the field access to represent the intended item-relative offset.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: coordinated changes across itCharItems.h, itarwinglaser.c, and types.h

smart_moves.text: The PR body records the specific hidden mismatch mechanism: omitting initial padding made `xE38` compile as `item+0xE18`, while objdiff fallback display masked the byte difference.
The author disclosed AI-assisted workflow and the need to clean extracted suggestions before PR submission, which is useful process evidence but not proof of code correctness by itself.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When replacing raw item-var offsets with typed union field access, recover the union arm layout from the base of `xDD4_itemVar`, not just from the first field used by the target function; missing leading padding can silently move every recovered field.
evidence_refs: pr_body: without `_pad0[0x20]`, field `xE38` compiled to `item+0xE18` instead of `item+0xE38`

decomp_lessons.lesson: For convention cleanup, pair accessor cleanup such as `GET_ITEM(gobj)` with the required inline header include rather than keeping object-layout pointer arithmetic.
Objdiff fallback display may not expose all byte-level mismatches for offset-sensitive struct recovery; raw byte comparison can reveal that a field access compiled to the wrong offset.
evidence_refs: pr_body notes: objdiff fallback display mode masked the hidden byte mismatch
pr_body notes: raw byte comparison revealed the actual difference

naming_conventions.name: itArwingLaser_ItemVars
naming_conventions.lesson: Item-vars structs in this area use the `it<Name>_ItemVars` naming pattern, matching nearby examples such as `itFoxLaser_ItemVars` shown in the diff context.

naming_conventions.name: arwinglaser
naming_conventions.lesson: The union arm name is lowercase item identity without underscores, consistent with adjacent members visible in the diff such as `foxblaster`, `foxillusion`, `foxlaser`, `freeze`, and `freezer`.

naming_conventions.name: xE38 and other offset-style fields
naming_conventions.lesson: Where semantic roles are not proven in the PR evidence, the recovered struct keeps address-style field names and offset comments rather than inventing semantic names.

naming_conventions.name: _pad0, _pad1, xE32_pad, xE34_pad, xE3C_pad
naming_conventions.lesson: Padding fields are explicitly marked as padding and tied to offset ranges or item offsets when layout bytes are known but semantics are not recovered.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: padding comments and field names in `itArwingLaser_ItemVars`

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use struct padding expressions based on item offsets to preserve field placement from the union base.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.details: `_pad0[0xDF4 - 0xDD4]` keeps `xDF4` and later fields aligned relative to `Item::xDD4_itemVar`; the PR body says missing this padding moved `xE38` to the wrong compiled offset.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use raw byte comparison when objdiff fallback display appears identical but a hidden mismatch is suspected.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.details: The PR body states raw byte comparison revealed an actual difference that objdiff fallback display masked.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Prefer typed item-var union access over raw `((u8*) item) + offset` reads once the correct union arm exists.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.details: The diff replaces `*(int*) ((u8*) item + 0xE38)` with `item->xDD4_itemVar.arwinglaser.xE38` after adding the union arm.

review_feedback.author: decomp-dev[bot]
review_feedback.text: Report for GALE01 from base `4a32477` to head `33d89f5` said `No changes`.

follow_up_queries: Find other item functions still using `*(Item**) ((u8*) gobj + 0x2C)` instead of `GET_ITEM(gobj)`.
Search `src/melee/it` for raw accesses to `((u8*) item + 0xE38)` or nearby `xDD4_itemVar` offsets.
Audit other `xDD4_itemVar` union arms for missing leading padding from item+0xDD4.
Check whether knowledge standards should mention objdiff fallback display masking byte mismatches for offset-sensitive struct layout.
Review PRs/comments around `it_802E8418` and `it_802E8784` because this PR body claimed they were already implemented in master while review feedback said mentioned functions were not committed.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Recover item-var struct padding from the xDD4 union base before replacing raw offsets
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2237 shows that replacing `*(int*) ((u8*) item + 0xE38)` with `item->xDD4_itemVar.arwinglaser.xE38` required an `itArwingLaser_ItemVars` layout whose first padding spans item+0xDD4 to item+0xDF4. The author reported that omitting `_pad0[0x20]` made `xE38` compile to `item+0xE18` instead of `item+0xE38`.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: itarwinglaser.c changed raw `item+0xE38` to typed field access

accepted_candidate_records.title: Keep offset-style names for unrecovered Arwing Laser item-vars fields
accepted_candidate_records.text: The new `itArwingLaser_ItemVars` fields use offset-style names such as `xE18`, `xE38`, `xE40`, and padding-marked names where semantics are not shown by the PR evidence.

source_update_candidates.title: Mention objdiff fallback display caveat for struct-offset mismatches
source_update_candidates.text: Consider adding a verifier note that for offset-sensitive struct/union recovery, objdiff fallback display may hide a byte mismatch; if a typed field access appears visually equivalent but field placement is uncertain, compare raw bytes or another exact diff view before accepting the layout.
evidence_refs: pr_body notes: objdiff fallback display mode masked a hidden byte mismatch
pr_body notes: raw byte comparison revealed the actual difference
source_update_candidates.reason: The PR provides a concrete author-reported pitfall that complements existing verification standards, but the slice lacks the underlying objdiff/raw-byte artifact, so curator should decide whether one PR body is enough for a global standards update.

rejection_notes: Do not promote the PR title/body claim about `it_802E8418` and `it_802E8784` beyond noting it as author text; issue comments say mentioned functions were not committed and the author acknowledged the description was bad.
Do not infer reviewer approval rationale or merge acceptance beyond the metadata state `MERGED`; the slice contains no review approvals or review comments.

Do not claim an independently verified match from raw bytes or objdiff logs; only the bot `No changes` comment and author-reported raw byte comparison are present.
